sutta: the buddha said monks is poverty a woeful thing for an immoral worldly person surely lord among replied and when among and when a man is poor needy in streets he gets into death is that woeful too surely lord and when he gets into death he borrows is that woeful too surely lord and when the bill falls due he pays not and they press him is that woeful too surely lord and when pressed he pays not and they beset him is that woeful too surely lord and when be said he pays not and they bind him tie him up is that woeful too surely lord thus monks poverty that borrowing being pressed beset and bound are all world wars for an immoral worldly person monks it is just the same for anyone who has no faith in wholesome states or skillful states is not conscientious about wholesome states has no fear of blame about wholesome states no energy for wholesome states no insight into wholesome states he is said to be poor needy in straits in the aryan discipline now that very man poor needy in straits wanting in faith conscientiousness fear of blame energy and insight concerning wholesome states works evilly in deep word and thought i call that his getting into death and to cloak his evil deeds he lays hold upon false hope let none know this of me he hopes let none know this of me he resolves let none know this of me he says let none know this of me he strives in act so likewise cloak his evil words his evil thoughts i call that his borrowing then his pious fellows in the holy life saith us this venerable sir acts in this sword carries on in this way i call that his being pressed then gone to forest tree root or lonely place evil and wholesome thoughts and attendant remorse pursue him i call that his being beset and that man monks poor and needy in straits having worked evilly deed word and thought on the breaking up of the body after death is bound in hell's bones or the bones of some animal's womb and i see no other single bondage monks so harsh so bitter such a bar to winning the unsurpassed peace from effort i mean hell's bonds or the bonds of an animal's womb that's the end of the suta
commentary: so the buddha is saying and just as a poor man because he is poor he gets into debt and then he's being chased by the debtors for payment and he cannot pay them and they time up then he's in a very sorrowful condition so in the same way the buddha said if a person does not know about wholesome states cannot differentiate between wholesome and unwholesome states that means he does not understand the law of karma then he is also like that poor man and he tries to hide his evil actions and then put other people criticize him and then he's being pressed being besetna and and even his thoughts and wholesome thoughts and rethoughts of remorse and pursue him and that person after death is born into the woeful plainsla now the 2 food plains are 3 the uh hell hell is the worst the second is the animal realm and the third is the ghost realm but here the buddha says i see no other single bondage monks so harsh so bitter such a bar to winning the unsurpassed peace from effort as hell's bonds or the bonds of an animal's womb here the buddha is saying that when a person falls into hell or into the animal realm that person stays there for a very long time a very long time and because that person has no chance of creating good marriedness it's very difficult to get out from that through states hell and the animal womb and that person stays there for a very long time and still the karma most of that evil karma is worked out now then only that person comes up the next futa is
